verified.name: Open Government, Singapore
verified.tagline: Live Singapore environment, transport and weather data, no key
verified.description: Singapore's national open-data platform exposes real-time government feeds such as air temperature, rainfall and traffic, all as plain JSON. No signup or key is needed, and responses are CORS-enabled for browser apps. Handy for dashboards that want fresh readings straight from official sensors.
verified.freeTier: Free — no API key required; limits not published

verified.name: Open Government, Taiwan
verified.tagline: Metadata for Taiwan's national open-data catalog, keyless
verified.description: Taiwan's government open-data portal offers a keyless REST API returning JSON metadata for each published dataset, including title, provider, license and update cadence. Fetch a dataset by its numeric id to see how the catalog describes it. Useful for indexing or exploring what Taiwanese agencies have released.

result.title: 金融機構基本資料查詢

verified.freeTier: Free — no API key required; limits not published
verified.dataLicense: Open Government Data License, Taiwan (portal code "1")

verified.name: iTunes Search
verified.tagline: Search Apple's catalog of music, apps, books and podcasts
verified.description: Apple's public Search API queries the iTunes and App Store catalog for songs, albums, apps, audiobooks, podcasts and more, returning rich JSON with artwork and store links. No key or signup is needed; you just pass a search term. Apple asks callers to keep request volume modest.
verified.rateLimit: Apple suggests keeping to roughly 20 requests/minute

verified.name: Songsterr
verified.tagline: Search guitar, bass and drum tabs by artist or title
verified.description: Songsterr's search endpoint returns JSON listings of songs matching a text pattern, each with a song id, artist, title and the available instrument tracks. No key is required for the search call. Good for looking up whether tabs exist for a given artist before deep-linking into the player.

verified.name: Wikipedia
verified.tagline: Query Wikipedia article text and metadata via MediaWiki
verified.description: The MediaWiki Action API powers Wikipedia and returns article content, extracts, links and metadata as JSON with no key needed. This example pulls a plaintext intro extract for a page title. MediaWiki asks callers to send a descriptive User-Agent and be reasonable with request volume.

27697009.title: API
27697009.extract: An application programming interface (API) is a connection between computers or between computer programs. …

verified.rateLimit: No published hard limit; requires a descriptive User-Agent
